perturbation.original_text: In return for the exclusive appointment of Agency by MICOA to sell its professional liability products listed on the &sbsp;attached Commission Schedule in Nevada, Agency agrees not to sell any competing professional liability products in Nevada, without the written consent of MICOA. Provided that, if a particular risk has been submitted to MICOA and MICOA has declined that risk, then Agency may search appropriate markets for placement of that risk, and may place that risk with another insurance company.
perturbation.changed_text: In return for the appointment of Agency by MICOA to sell its professional liability products in Nevada, Agency agrees not to sell any competing professional liability products in Nevada, without the written consent of MICOA. Provided that, if a particular risk has been submitted to MICOA and MICOA has declined that risk, then Agency may search appropriate markets for placement of that risk, and may place that risk with another insurance company.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'exclusive' it creates an in-text contradiction because Section H: POTENTIAL OPPORTUNITIES states, 'Agency and MICOA agree that Agency may be offered the opportunity to support MICOA's workers' compensation, and its other nonphysician professional liability or product programs in Nevada when MICOA proceeds with related marketing plans.' This implies MICOA may offer these opportunities but because the agency is no longer exclusive it can be given to another agency. This introduces uncertainty because it gives MICOA the discretion to offer other agencies sales territories that were originally exclusive.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other Programs. Agency and MICOA agree that Agency may be offered the opportunity to support MICOA's workers' compensation, and its other nonphysician professional liability or product programs in Nevada when MICOA proceeds with related marketing plans. Such plans may also include Agency's involvement in sales of MICOA commercial and personal products. Appropriate agreements must be negotiated separately from this agreement for each such product, and for each such territory, including but not limited to Nevada.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants Agency an 'exclusive appointment' to sell MICOA products in Nevada. The changed text removes 'exclusive,' meaning MICOA could appoint other agencies in Nevada. The contradicted text states that 'Agency may be offered the opportunity to support MICOA's workers' compensation...' This implies the agency *could* be given additional opportunities, but removing exclusivity means those opportunities could also be given to other agencies, creating a direct contradiction because exclusivity assures that this opportunity is only given to Agency. This contradiction creates uncertainty about MICOA's obligations regarding which agency will support these opportunities, which is a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Commissions on premiums shall be paid to the Agency within 30 business days of the month in which such premiums are received and recorded by MICOA, subject to deduction by MICOA of any return commissions due from the Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: Commissions on premiums shall be paid to the Agency, subject to deduction by MICOA of any return commissions due from the Agency.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'within 30 business days of the month in which such premiums are received and recorded by MICOA' it is unclaear when the commissions will be paid and causes an in-text contradiction because Exhibit A: AGENCY AGREEMENT SCHEDULE OF COMMISSIONS AND WRITTEN PREMIUM states, 'New Business Policies: 12% of the annual premium, Renewal Policies: 12% of the annual premium' This implies the commissions will be paid since it is specified for new and renewal policies, but does not provide the required timeline of when it will be paid. This creates uncertainty because the agency will not know how long it will take to receive payments.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT A AGENCY AGREEMENT SCHEDULE OF COMMISSIONS AND WRITTEN PREMIUM New Business Policies: 12% of the annual premium Renewal Policies: 12% of the annual premium Appointed agents who are not a party to a current MICOA agency contract and/or are not affiliated with an agency which has an agency contract will receive a 1% commission rate for all lines of business stated above. Commission will decrease by .5% effective 10/1/99 as part of a repayment program under a project memorandum dated 4/7/99. This decrease will stay in effect until SC&W reaches $10MM in premium or at a maximum of 10 years.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a timeframe for commission payments (30 business days). The changed text removes this timeframe. Exhibit A specifies commission percentages but is silent on payment timing. The omission in the changed text creates a contradiction because Exhibit A does not define the payment timeframe, which was previously established, creating uncertainty and potential conflict as to when payment should be rendered. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction where essential language is removed.

perturbation.original_text: The Agency will maintain valid errors and omissions insurance, with minimum limits of $1,000,000 per incident, and a fidelity and electronic crime policy through an insurer, both of which shall contain terms and limits of coverage acceptable to MICOA covering the Agency's solicitors and each of its employees. The Agency shall provide MICOA a copy of each policy; doing so on a regular and current basis shall be a precondition to all of Agency's rights under this Agreement, including but not limited to the payment of all earned commissions.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agency will maintain errors and omissions insurance, and a fidelity and electronic crime policy through an insurer covering the Agency's solicitors and each of its employees. The Agency shall provide MICOA a copy of each policy; doing so on a regular and current basis shall be a precondition to all of Agency's rights under this Agreement, including but not limited to the payment of all earned commissions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'valid', 'with minimum limits of $1,000,000 per incident,', and 'both of which shall contain terms and limits of coverage acceptable to MICOA' creates an in-text contradiction because Section G: INDEMNIFICATION states, 'Agency shall indemnify and hold Agency harmless against any act or omission of the Agency, except to the extent MICOA has caused, compounded, or contributed to such error.' It creates a contradiction because the the agency can have any errors and ommissions insurance without any requirements, making indemnification on the policy vague and uncertain for both the agency and MICOA since the level of errors and ommissions required is not specified. This creates uncertainty because the indemnification clause will not be able to be properly enforced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Agency shall indemnify and hold Agency harmless against any act or omission of the Agency, except to the extent MICOA has caused, compounded, or contributed to such error.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that the agency must maintain 'valid errors and omissions insurance, with minimum limits of $1,000,000 per incident, and ... terms and limits of coverage acceptable to MICOA.' By removing these requirements in the changed text, the agency could have a policy with minimal or no coverage. This directly contradicts the clause stating 'Agency shall indemnify and hold Agency harmless against any act or omission of the Agency...', because the indemnification is now uncertain and unenforceable due to the lack of specific insurance requirements. The original text provided a clear basis for indemnification through the required insurance policy. The changed text removes this basis, creating a significant contradiction regarding the extent of the agency's liability and MICOA's protection.
